message: Stage -1.2 drafted angle=0 v1 mode=cold-start runner=codex/gpt-5.5 status=completed

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): case 6b — Stage 0.5 reject is conjecture-level (novelty floor): positive Theorem 1 is a routine soundness substitution from ordinary ID and the negative Theorem 2 refutes only the in-run source-cover conjecture without naming a specific published estimator/algorithm/workflow. Correctness/structure both pass; the defect is in the angle itself, so Stage 0 re-derivation on the same .tex would burn budget.

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): case 6b + regression pattern (a) — same novelty-floor flag persists across all three attempts; the producer's own note self-disclaims flagship status (lines 213-221, 596-603), and the reviewer's reject is at the conjecture level (the chain-refined source-cover closure is structurally a finite-chain-rule certificate inside an established framework, not an open-regime result). Re-deriving the same .tex would burn budget — the artifact needs a new angle that names a specific published estimator/algorithm to refute or strictly extend, or a separation/equivalence frontier against DoSearch/gID/Lee-Ghassami-Shpitser. Override of stage_0 heuristic: defect is conjecture-level, not derivation-level.
